title: Accelerated graduate degree programs.
subject: AGENCIES; Commission for Higher Education (CHE)
HEALTH CARE PROFESSIONALS; Behavioral Health
HEALTH CARE PROFESSIONALS; Licensing (Including Behavioral and Mental Health)
HEALTH; Behavioral Health; Generally
HIGHER EDUCATION; Academics and Curriculum
HIGHER EDUCATION; Colleges and Universities (Including Ivy Tech)
SOCIAL SERVICES; Social Workers
TECHNOLOGY; Communication (Including Internet, Phone Lines, Print Media, Radio, and Television)

abstracts.abstract: Requires each state educational institution that offers a two year graduate degree program in mental or behavioral health to evaluate the graduate degree program to determine the feasibility of providing the program in a specifically structured manner to allow full-time students to complete the program within: (1) one year; or (2) five years if the program is combined with a baccalaureate degree program. Requires certain state educational institutions to offer at least one graduate degree program in one or more certain disciplines that is structured to allow full-time students to complete the program within: (1) one year; or (2) five years if the program is combined with a baccalaureate degree program. Requires certain state educational institutions to report to the commission for higher education (commission) regarding the graduate degree programs. Requires the commission to post the submitted reports on the commission's website.

actions.description: First reading: referred to Committee on Education and Career Development
Senator Goode added as second author
Senator Crider added as third author
